title: rubus glandulosus subsp. cupanianus
intro: Rubus glandulosus subsp. cupanianus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Nepalese raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus glandulosus plant, a type of raspberry native to the Himalayan region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. Rubus glandulosus subsp. cupanianus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and has been used to make jams and preserves, although its use as a food or medicine should be consulted with a healthcare professional before use.
